howItWorks.intro: This scholarship calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Net Cost = (Tuition + Room & Board) – (Scholarships + Federal Aid)
Total Cost = Annual Net Cost × Years
Average aid covers 30-60% of total cost
howItWorks.outro: Educational costs and financial aid vary by institution. Contact schools directly for current figures.

faq.question: What is the average scholarship amount?
faq.answer: The average private scholarship is $5,000-10,000 per year. Institutional merit scholarships average $7,000-15,000. Full-ride scholarships cover everything but are rare — less than 0.1% of students receive one.

faq.question: Do scholarships affect financial aid?
faq.answer: Outside scholarships may reduce need-based aid. Many schools reduce loans first (good), but some reduce grants (bad). Always ask the financial aid office about their scholarship displacement policy.

faq.question: What is the average net price of college?
faq.answer: After all aid, the average net price is $15,000-20,000/year at public universities and $25,000-35,000 at private colleges. This is significantly less than the sticker price that schools advertise.

faq.question: Is the Scholarship Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard education and learning formulas trusted by students and educators. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Scholarship Calculator?
sections.body: The Scholarship Calculator is a free online tool designed for students and educators who need quick, accurate calculations in the education and learning space. By entering your annual tuition + fees, room & board, scholarships / grants, you get instant results including annual net cost, total 4-year net cost, % covered by aid. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting annual net cost right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In education and learning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by students and educators worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your education with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your annual tuition + fees and need to find the right annual net cost. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Annual Tuition + Fees
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your annual tuition + fees — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: room & board, scholarships / grants, federal aid, years of study. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your annual net cost, total 4-year net cost, % covered by aid. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your annual tuition + fees before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both annual net cost and total 4-year net cost — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to plan your education.
If you're unsure about your years of study, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
